items.text: I enjoy building working models or doing hands-on experiments in the lab.
I'd rather fix a broken device myself than take it to a repair shop.
I find myself drawn to workshops that involve tools, machines, or physical materials.
I like tinkering with electronics, circuits, or hardware setups in my free time.
I'd enjoy a summer internship where I work with physical equipment or field installations.
I prefer assignments that produce a tangible output over purely theoretical ones.
Setting up a home lab or Raspberry Pi project sounds exciting to me.
I enjoy sports, outdoor activities, or physical challenges more than desk work.
I often read beyond what's assigned because I'm curious about the topic.
I enjoy figuring out why something works, not just how to use it.
I find research papers or technical deep-dives more interesting than most people do.
I like solving puzzles, brain teasers, or logic problems in my spare time.
I'd rather investigate the root cause of a bug than just apply a quick fix.
I enjoy subjects that require deep analytical thinking over memorization.
I'd be interested in a career that involves continuous learning and research.
Collecting and analyzing data to find patterns excites me.
I enjoy designing posters, presentations, or creative projects for college events.
I often come up with unconventional solutions that others wouldn't think of.
I enjoy writing — blogs, stories, poems, or even long social media posts.
I'm drawn to courses or electives that involve design, media, or creative expression.
I'd rather build a creative UI/UX for a project than optimize its backend performance.
I enjoy photography, video editing, or creating visual content.
I like working on projects where I can express my own style or vision.
I find myself modding games, creating digital art, or experimenting with music production.
I enjoy tutoring classmates or explaining difficult concepts to friends.
I'm usually the person people come to when they need advice or support.
I'd enjoy volunteering for a social cause or community service project.
I prefer group projects where I can collaborate and help others contribute.
I'd be interested in mentoring juniors or being a student council member.
I feel satisfied when I help someone learn something new.
I'm drawn to careers in education, counseling, healthcare, or social work.
I'd rather resolve a conflict in a team than focus only on my own deliverable.
I enjoy organizing college events, fests, or club activities.
I often find myself taking charge in group projects, even when not assigned as leader.
I like pitching ideas — in class, in competitions, or to friends.
I've thought seriously about starting a business or side project for profit.
I'd rather lead a team in a hackathon than just code alone.
I enjoy debating, public speaking, or persuading others.
I'm energized by networking events, meetups, or professional conferences.
I'd enjoy a role where my income depends on my ability to sell or influence.
I keep my notes, files, and folders well-organized and easy to find.
I prefer assignments with clear instructions and defined deliverables.
I enjoy tasks that require attention to detail — data entry, proofreading, formatting.
I like maintaining schedules, to-do lists, or spreadsheets to track progress.
I'd be comfortable in a role that involves following standard procedures and compliance rules.
I find it satisfying to organize an event's logistics — budgets, timelines, checklists.
I prefer working in structured environments over chaotic or fast-changing ones.
I'd rather document a process correctly than improvise on the fly.

attention_checks.text: I have never used any electronic device in my life.
I can read and understand the questions in this assessment.
